name: Gliding
description: A controlled aerial traversal state that reduces fall velocity and uses lift, drag, stamina, wind, or pitch input to extend airborne travel.
design_notes: Gliding changes how players read height, distance, wind, and landing risk. Define how gliding starts, which fall states permit it, how lift and drag are applied, what resource drains, and how landing transitions restore grounded locomotion. Camera comfort is central because gliding often combines vertical motion, long sightlines, and steering corrections.
design_purpose: Let players convert height into distance and route planning.
Make vertical level design feel expansive without requiring flight everywhere.
Create skill expression through pitch, wind, stamina, and landing choices.
player_fantasy: The player feels light and controlled, stretching a fall into a deliberate path across open space.

parameters.name: glide_lift_coefficient
parameters.description: Lift multiplier applied while glide state is active.

parameters.name: glide_stamina_drain_units_per_second
parameters.description: Resource drain applied during sustained glide.

parameters.name: glide_pitch_limit_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed nose-up or nose-down steering angle.

parameters.name: landing_flare_window_ms
parameters.description: Input window that softens landing or cancels glide cleanly.

parameters.name: wind_response_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used when wind zones push or lift the glider.

edge_cases: Glide activation near a ceiling or wall needs a blocked-start result instead of clipping the character upward.
Landing on moving platforms, slopes, or water needs a clear transition into grounded, sliding, or swimming states.
Wind zones should not stack into infinite lift unless the level is authored for it.
Networked gliding needs server validation for altitude, stamina, and wind samples used by the client.
common_bugs: Fall velocity cap applies after collision and lets the player sink through thin floors.
Stamina drains while glide input is held even after landing.
Camera pitch follows the glider too aggressively and creates motion discomfort.
Wind zones use render-order sampling and produce different paths across clients.
balancing_notes: Glide availability changes how every high point functions as a route start.
Resource drain and fall velocity cap determine whether gliding is exploration, escape, or precision traversal.
Strong wind interaction can make authored landing targets unreliable unless telegraphed.
accessibility_notes: Provide camera comfort options for pitch lock, reduced sway, and toned-down field-of-view shifts.
Offer aim or steering assists for long glides that require sustained fine analog control.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit airborne glide state and apply lift, drag, and fall_velocity_cap in FixedUpdate before CharacterController or Rigidbody collision. Landing detection should atomically clear glide flags and stamina drain.
implementation_notes.godot: Update glide velocity inside _physics_process and sample Area-based wind volumes deterministically. Use move_and_slide collision results to choose landing, water entry, or continued airborne state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a custom CharacterMovement mode for glide so gravity scale, air control, and replicated movement stay coherent. Server authority should validate glide start height, stamina, and wind volume membership.
implementation_notes.web: Store glide_state with pitch input, lift coefficient, wind zone id, and stamina ledger. Resolve vertical velocity in the fixed simulation loop, then render wing or cape animation from committed state.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses gliding to convert altitude into travel routes.

example_games.title: Just Cause 3
example_games.note: Uses wingsuit-style gliding for aerial traversal.
